title: Promos
weakest_section: 03:00-04:00 range with awkward Jim-Pam phone conversation

jokes.text: It's porn. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Character giving euphemistic explanation of Fifty Shades of Grey
jokes.punchline: Jim cuts through with blunt, matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - cutting through awkwardness with brutal honesty. Economy of language is excellent.

jokes.text: Be careful. I pulled my mom's dog off a pillow once, and I got bit.
jokes.setup: Discussion about confronting Phyllis about inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Meredith shares disturbing personal anecdote as 'helpful' advice
jokes.explanation: Peak Meredith - oversharing inappropriate family details. The specificity of 'pulled my mom's dog off a pillow' makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Just ignore her. Sooner or later, she'll finish.
jokes.setup: Discussion about how to handle Phyllis's situation
jokes.punchline: Stanley's pragmatic advice has unintended sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Classic Stanley - pragmatic to the point of accidental innuendo. Perfect character voice and economical delivery.

jokes.text: Oh, Meredith! Good lord.
jokes.setup: Stanley's 'she'll finish' comment
jokes.punchline: Angela's horrified reaction to the implication
jokes.explanation: Standard Angela prudish reaction. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Turns out she's allowed to have feelings of sexual arousal. Doesn't become a violation till she physically acts on it.
jokes.setup: Office discussing firing Phyllis for inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Toby delivers legalistic HR distinction about arousal vs action
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby - turning uncomfortable situation into bureaucratic policy discussion. The absurdity of HR regulations on arousal is genuinely funny.

jokes.text: Well, there you go. That's muy caliente.
jokes.setup: Learning Phyllis is listening to Fifty Shades
jokes.punchline: Oscar's academic/pretentious response to office sexual situation
jokes.explanation: Oscar being unnecessarily sophisticated about a crass situation. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: It's okay, guys. She's no longer horny.
jokes.setup: Angela taking away Phyllis's audiobook
jokes.punchline: Prim Angela forced to use crude language matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - Angela forced to say 'horny' clinically. The contrast with her usual prudishness makes it very funny.

jokes.text: Long-term tractor leases can put pressure on a relationship.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking for relationship advice about farm equipment
jokes.punchline: Clark treating tractor leases as legitimate relationship concern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - treating farm equipment as relationship milestone. The sincerity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: God, a lot of my buddies are going through the same thing right now. Something in the air.
jokes.setup: Tractor lease relationship pressure
jokes.punchline: Clark suggesting widespread tractor lease relationship epidemic
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd premise. 'Something in the air' treats tractor leases like a seasonal romance plague.

jokes.text: Cece has been calling me Pamela. Like, four times this week.
jokes.setup: Pam talking to Jim about their daughter
jokes.punchline: Daughter using formal name suggests distance/formality
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about parent-child relationships. Relatable but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: What's going on? Did Gangnam Style put out a new song?
jokes.setup: Everyone crowded around computer excitedly
jokes.punchline: Andy's outdated reference to explain viral video excitement
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference humor. Shows Andy's cluelessness but relies on recognition rather than wit.

jokes.text: You go to the bathroom for 45 minutes, and everything changes.
jokes.setup: Kevin returning to find documentary promo has aired
jokes.punchline: Kevin's admission of extremely long bathroom breaks
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin - casually admitting to absurdly long bathroom breaks. The specific '45 minutes' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh! I always thought we were, like, specimens in a human zoo.
jokes.setup: Learning they're in a documentary
jokes.punchline: Kevin's alternate theory about their situation
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on the show's premise. Kevin's confusion creates unexpected philosophical insight about reality TV.

jokes.text: You fell in love with that hair? Really?
jokes.setup: Pam and Jim watching old footage of themselves
jokes.punchline: Pam incredulous at Jim's past appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor about past appearances. Relatable but predictable.

jokes.text: I must have really connected with this guy. I mean, that's a guy's name, right? Chobblegobbler?
jokes.setup: Andy reading online comments about his banjo playing
jokes.punchline: Andy's sincere belief that 'Chobblegobbler' is a real name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy naivety - taking obvious fake username seriously. The absurdity of 'Chobblegobbler' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Nice to meet you. Eat fresh.
jokes.setup: Jim meeting Ryan Howard (baseball player)
jokes.punchline: Jim randomly adding Subway slogan to introduction
jokes.explanation: Establishes running gag with Subway slogan. Absurd but not particularly clever on its own.

jokes.text: I bet she'll eat 'em right out of your hand with those big, strong teeth.
jokes.setup: Dwight talking about Esther liking apples and carrots
jokes.punchline: Jim treating Esther like a horse based on her diet
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim prank mentality - making Dwight's girlfriend sound like livestock. The horse imagery is well-executed.

jokes.text: I think they're from the forest where we harvest our paper.
jokes.setup: Dwight's rural-looking guests arriving
jokes.punchline: Jim's fake-innocent comment about their rustic appearance
jokes.explanation: Good Jim mockery disguised as innocent observation. The paper company connection adds workplace humor.

jokes.text: I guess men find Esther attractive. I mean, if there are chubby chasers, then there are men that like that thing.
jokes.setup: Angela in talking head about Dwight's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Angela's cattily judgmental assessment disguised as understanding
jokes.explanation: Peak Angela cattiness - being judgmental while pretending to be open-minded. Character-appropriate cruelty.

jokes.text: It's called playing the subtext.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining how saying 'Eat fresh' makes people think of Subway
jokes.punchline: Jim using pretentious acting terminology for obvious advertising slogan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Subway gag. Jim's fake intellectual approach to obvious advertising makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Cool guy'? 'Dumpster man.' Cool. Superhero.
jokes.setup: Learning his Danish nickname means 'Dumpster man'
jokes.punchline: Creed's positive interpretation of being called garbage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - finding the positive in being called trash. His delusional optimism is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: '3:00 p.m. girl.' What? Why would they... Wait a second. Wait a second.
jokes.setup: Learning her Danish nickname
jokes.punchline: Erin's dawning realization of what '3:00 p.m. girl' implies
jokes.explanation: Good slow-burn realization moment. Erin's confusion turning to horror as she figures it out.

jokes.text: Yes, Erin. They film us at night when we're sleeping 'cause that makes great TV.
jokes.setup: Erin asking if they're filmed while sleeping
jokes.punchline: Oscar's sarcastic dismissal of paranoid question
jokes.explanation: Classic Oscar condescension. The sarcasm is character-appropriate but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: I sneezed into my hands without using Purell and then dipped into the candy jar.
jokes.setup: Everyone worried about what was filmed secretly
jokes.punchline: Prim Angela confessing to disgusting hygiene violation
jokes.explanation: Great character subversion - germaphobic Angela admitting to spreading germs. The hypocrisy is perfectly cringe.

jokes.text: My first week here, I sneezed directly into the candy jar because I thought I'd get more screen time as a villain.
jokes.setup: Angela's candy jar confession
jokes.punchline: Clark's intentionally villainous candy contamination for camera time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on reality TV behavior. The calculated grossness for screen time is both funny and disturbing.

jokes.text: Mr. Brueger, are you trying to take advantage of me because I'm interested in your daughter?
jokes.setup: Tractor negotiation with girlfriend's father
jokes.punchline: Dwight's blunt accusation during business deal
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight directness in uncomfortable situation. The blunt question during negotiations is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Something about the wiring.
jokes.setup: Andy's mom calling about TV repair
jokes.punchline: Clearly lying about why TVs need to stay off during documentary airing
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Andy's family shame. The transparent lie about wiring is believably absurd.

jokes.text: He's not hawt. He's gay.
jokes.setup: Andy getting positive comments online
jokes.punchline: Cruel anonymous comment deflating Andy's ego
jokes.explanation: Generic internet cruelty. Sets up Andy's defensive response but isn't particularly clever on its own.

jokes.text: Dear JasonJasonJason, it's me, Andy. Nice name. Not!
jokes.setup: Andy responding to mean comment
jokes.punchline: Andy's childish 'Not!' comeback to anonymous troll
jokes.explanation: Andy's immaturity on display. The dated 'Not!' reference shows his juvenile response to criticism.

jokes.text: He is butt.
jokes.setup: Online comment thread about Andy
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely phrased insult that sounds like broken English
jokes.explanation: The awkward phrasing 'He is butt' is funnier than standard insults. Sounds like bad translation or child.

jokes.text: These chicks are way too hot to be into us.
jokes.setup: Clark and Dwight discussing their romantic situations
jokes.punchline: Clark's blunt assessment of their attractiveness mismatch
jokes.explanation: Self-aware observation about dating leagues. Honest but not particularly original.

jokes.text: No woman would ever want a man who doesn't know what an auger is.
jokes.setup: Clark admitting he doesn't know what an auger is
jokes.punchline: Dwight's farm equipment knowledge as masculine requirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight worldview - farm equipment knowledge as dating prerequisite. The specificity makes it absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: He was dressed like Ronald Reagan! Well, he kissed like Jack Kennedy.
jokes.setup: Angela talking about kissing the Senator
jokes.punchline: Angela's unexpectedly sexy historical comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - prim Angela being unexpectedly sultry with presidential reference. The historical specificity is perfect.

jokes.text: Pretty much everything.
jokes.setup: Pam asking how much private stuff was filmed
jokes.punchline: Brian's matter-of-fact admission of total surveillance
jokes.explanation: Effective deadpan reveal of documentary's invasiveness. The economy of 'pretty much everything' is chilling and funny.

jokes.text: A bunch of hot women go, 'Oh, yeah!'
jokes.setup: Reading from Ryan's screenplay
jokes.punchline: Juvenile fantasy writing in supposed professional script
jokes.explanation: Standard bad screenplay humor. The juvenile fantasy is cringe-worthy but predictable.

jokes.text: A Scranton nine, but, yeah, point taken.
jokes.setup: Dwight saying Angela was a nine who rejected him
jokes.punchline: Dwight's geographic qualifier lowering the rating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant local adjustment to attractiveness scale. The geographic qualifier is both funny and realistic to character.

jokes.text: Maybe we're the kind of guys who end up with a tractor that's already been rode hard and put away muddy.
jokes.setup: Dwight and Clark accepting they can't get perfect women
jokes.punchline: Dwight using used tractor as metaphor for relationship prospects
jokes.explanation: Exceptional metaphor combining farm equipment with dating. The specificity of 'rode hard and put away muddy' is perfectly Dwight and highly quotable.

jokes.text: That shady grove out by Willard's Pond.
jokes.setup: Esther telling Mr. Brueger where sun don't shine
jokes.punchline: Literal location instead of anatomical reference
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of common phrase. The literal interpretation of 'where the sun don't shine' is unexpected and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Post hole digging.
jokes.setup: Clark asking what an auger is used for
jokes.punchline: Simple answer to earlier relationship-destroying ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff. The simple answer deflates the earlier dramatic importance Dwight placed on auger knowledge.

jokes.text: Yes, and I cheated on you with Dwight.
jokes.setup: Angela leaving voicemail for Senator about documentary
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of affair in cheerful voicemail tone
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - devastating personal revelation delivered in chipper voicemail tone. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, TexasPoonTappa and JasonJasonJason and all the rest of you haters out there, check this out.
jokes.setup: Andy getting increasingly angry at online comments
jokes.punchline: Andy addressing anonymous trolls by their ridiculous usernames
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Andy's internet rage. Taking trolls seriously enough to address them personally is perfect cringe.

jokes.text: I hope you got sound on everything. I'd love a DVD of that.
jokes.setup: Meredith and someone else in compromising position
jokes.punchline: Meredith requesting copy of her own inappropriate footage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith - zero shame about inappropriate behavior, actually wants to commemorate it. Character integration is flawless.
